article_meta.title: Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell on June 25, 2025, in Washington, DC

reasoning: Mostly neutral and uses attribution for evaluative claims. A few mildly loaded phrases echo the article’s tone (e.g., “unprecedented confrontation,” “forcefully rejected,” “ironically”) but do not dominate the summary.

custom_reasoning.spin: Uses a few interpretive terms (“unprecedented,” “forcefully,” “uphill battle”) but most claims are attributed.
custom_reasoning.unsubstantiated_claims: Key assertions (threat to independence, expert skepticism) are tied to Powell, central bankers, or legal experts.
custom_reasoning.opinion_as_fact: Occasional subjective framing (“unprecedented,” “ironically”) appears in the model’s voice but limited.
custom_reasoning.sensationalism: Tone is measured; no dramatic amplification beyond mild descriptors.
custom_reasoning.mudslinging: No personal attacks by the model.
custom_reasoning.mind_reading: No claims about motives without attribution.
custom_reasoning.slant: Emphasizes defense of Fed independence; still includes Trump allies’ allegations and GOP skepticism.
custom_reasoning.bias_by_omission: Minor details (e.g., investors’ reactions) are not included but core perspectives are present in the summary.
custom_reasoning.subjective_adjectives: A few subjective adjectives/adverbs present but sparse.
custom_reasoning.word_choice: Politically neutral vocabulary overall; charged phrases are attributed.
custom_reasoning.negativity_bias: Balance of risks and support mirrors the source.
custom_reasoning.elite_populist_bias: Slight deference to expert/central bank viewpoints, consistent with source.
custom_reasoning.framing_neutrality: Significantly more neutral than the opinion piece, with clear attributions.
custom_reasoning.perspective_completeness: Sources list omits a distinct perspective mentioned (Congressional Republicans), though reflected in the summary.
